content:
# Exodus Chapter 23 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios ~45v–46r (PDF pp. 89–90); Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 65–66 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_023_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_023_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_023_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `EXO_023_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

> **MANUSCRIPT OBSERVATION — CONCLUSION OF THE BOOK OF THE COVENANT:** The Leningrad Codex presents Exodus 23 in standard prose column format with full Masoretic vocalization and cantillation. This chapter concludes the Book of the Covenant (Sefer ha-Berit) that began at 20:22, moving through four distinct sections: justice and integrity in legal proceedings (vv. 1–9), sabbatical rhythms of rest (vv. 10–13), the three pilgrimage festivals (vv. 14–19), and YHWH's promises concerning the angel, conquest, and covenant fidelity (vv. 20–33). The chapter transitions from casuistic and apodictic law into prophetic-promissory speech as YHWH shifts from legislator to warrior-guarantor. The Vaticanus manuscript (pp. 65–66) presents the LXX rendering in continuous uncial script across three columns per page; the LXX versification aligns with the MT throughout this chapter.

---

## Laws Concerning Justice and Integrity (vv. 1–3)

**1** You shall not lift up [lo tissa] a report [shema] of emptiness [shav]. You shall not place [al tashet] your hand [yadekha] with [im] the wicked [ha-rasha] to be [lihyot] a witness [ed] of violence [khamas].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לֹא תִשָּׂא שֵׁמַע שָׁוְא אַל־תָּשֶׁת יָדְךָ עִם־רָשָׁע לִהְיֹת עֵד חָמָס. **Key terms:** שֵׁמַע שָׁוְא (shema shav) = "a report of emptiness/falsehood" — shema from שׁמע (shama, "to hear"); shav = "emptiness, falsehood, worthlessness." עֵד חָמָס (ed khamas) = "a witness of violence/injustice" — one who testifies falsely to cause harm. LXX: οὐ παραδέξῃ ἀκοὴν ματαίαν — ἀκοή ματαία ("vain report") for shema shav.

**2** You shall not be [lo tihyeh] after [akharei] the many [rabbim] for evil [le-ra'ot], and you shall not answer [ve-lo ta'aneh] upon [al] a dispute [riv] to turn aside [lintot] after [akharei] the many [rabbim] to pervert [le-hattot] [justice].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לֹא־תִהְיֶה אַחֲרֵי־רַבִּים לְרָעֹת וְלֹא־תַעֲנֶה עַל־רִב לִנְטֹת אַחֲרֵי רַבִּים לְהַטֹּת. **Key terms:** אַחֲרֵי־רַבִּים (akharei-rabbim) = "after the many" — warning against mob justice. לִנְטֹת (lintot) = qal infinitive of נטה (natah, "to stretch, turn aside, incline") — to bend one's testimony toward the majority. לְהַטֹּת (le-hattot) = hiphil infinitive of נטה — "to cause to turn aside, to pervert." LXX: οὐκ ἔσῃ μετὰ πλειόνων ἐπὶ κακίᾳ — πλείονες ("the greater number") for rabbim.

**3** And a poor man [ve-dal] you shall not favor [lo tehdar] in his dispute [be-rivo].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְדָל לֹא תֶהְדַּר בְּרִיבוֹ. **Key terms:** דָל (dal) = "poor, weak, low" — from דלל (dalal, "to be low, hang down"). תֶהְדַּר (tehdar) = qal imperfect of הדר (hadar, "to honor, adorn, show partiality") — "you shall [not] favor." Justice must be impartial even toward the poor. LXX: καὶ πένητα οὐκ ἐλεήσεις ἐν κρίσει — ἐλεέω ("show pity") for tehdar; a stronger term than the MT's "honor."

---

## Laws Concerning the Enemy's Animal (vv. 4–5)

**4** If [ki] you encounter [tifga] the ox [shor] of your enemy [oyivkha] or [o] his donkey [khamoro] going astray [to'eh], you shall surely return it [hashev teshivennu] to him [lo].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כִּי תִפְגַּע שׁוֹר אֹיִבְךָ אוֹ חֲמֹרוֹ תֹּעֶה הָשֵׁב תְּשִׁיבֶנּוּ לוֹ. **Key terms:** תִפְגַּע (tifga) = qal imperfect of פגע (paga, "to encounter, meet, fall upon") — an unexpected meeting. אֹיִבְךָ (oyivkha) = "your enemy" — personal enmity does not suspend the duty of care. הָשֵׁב תְּשִׁיבֶנּוּ (hashev teshivennu) = infinitive absolute + hiphil imperfect of שׁוב — "returning you shall return it." LXX: ἐὰν δὲ συναντήσῃς τῷ βοῒ τοῦ ἐχθροῦ σου — συναντάω ("meet with") for tifga.

**5** If [ki] you see [tir'eh] the donkey [khamor] of the one who hates you [sona'akha] lying down [rovets] under [takhat] its burden [masa'o], and you would refrain [ve-khadalta] from loosening [me-azov] [it] for him [lo] — you shall surely loosen [azov ta'azov] [it] with him [immo].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כִּי־תִרְאֶה חֲמוֹר שֹׂנַאֲךָ רֹבֵץ תַּחַת מַשָּׂאוֹ וְחָדַלְתָּ מֵעֲזֹב לוֹ עָזֹב תַּעֲזֹב עִמּוֹ. **Key terms:** שֹׂנַאֲךָ (sona'akha) = "the one who hates you" — qal participle of שׂנא (sane, "to hate"); a personal adversary. רֹבֵץ (rovets) = qal participle of רבץ (ravats, "to lie down, crouch") — the animal has collapsed. עָזֹב תַּעֲזֹב (azov ta'azov) = infinitive absolute + qal imperfect of עזב (azav, "to leave, release, loosen") — "loosening you shall loosen." LXX: ἐὰν δὲ ἴδῃς τὸ ὑποζύγιον τοῦ ἐχθροῦ σου — ὑποζύγιον ("beast of burden") for khamor.

---

## Justice for the Poor and the Sojourner (vv. 6–9)

**6** You shall not pervert [lo tatteh] the justice [mishpat] of your poor [evyonekha] in his dispute [be-rivo].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לֹא תַטֶּה מִשְׁפַּט אֶבְיֹנְךָ בְּרִיבוֹ. **Key terms:** תַטֶּה (tatteh) = hiphil imperfect of נטה (natah, "to stretch, turn aside") — "you shall [not] turn aside, pervert." אֶבְיֹנְךָ (evyonekha) = "your poor, your needy one" — from אביון (evyon, "needy, destitute"); a different word than dal (v. 3). LXX: οὐ διαστρέψεις κρίμα πένητος ἐν κρίσει αὐτοῦ — διαστρέφω ("distort, pervert") for tatteh.

**7** From a word [mi-devar] of falsehood [sheqer] you shall keep far [tirkhaq], and the innocent [ve-naqi] and the righteous [ve-tsaddiq] you shall not kill [al taharog], for [ki] I will not declare righteous [lo atsdiq] the wicked [rasha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads מִדְּבַר־שֶׁקֶר תִּרְחָק וְנָקִי וְצַדִּיק אַל־תַּהֲרֹג כִּי לֹא־אַצְדִּיק רָשָׁע. **Key terms:** מִדְּבַר־שֶׁקֶר (mi-devar-sheqer) = "from a word/matter of falsehood" — sheqer = deceptive falsehood. תִּרְחָק (tirkhaq) = qal imperfect of רחק (rakhaq, "to be far") — "you shall be far [from]." לֹא־אַצְדִּיק רָשָׁע (lo atsdiq rasha) = "I will not declare righteous the wicked" — YHWH speaks in first person; hiphil of צדק. LXX: ἀπὸ παντὸς ῥήματος ἀδίκου ἀποστήσῃ — ἀφίστημι ("stand away from") for tirkhaq.

**8** And a bribe [ve-shokhad] you shall not take [lo tiqqakh], for [ki] the bribe [ha-shokhad] blinds [ye'avver] the seeing [piqqekhim] and perverts [vi-sallef] the words [divrei] of the righteous [tsaddiqim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשֹׁחַד לֹא תִקָּח כִּי הַשֹּׁחַד יְעַוֵּר פִּקְחִים וִיסַלֵּף דִּבְרֵי צַדִּיקִים. **Key terms:** שֹׁחַד (shokhad) = "bribe, gift" — from שׁחד (shakhad, "to give a present"). יְעַוֵּר (ye'avver) = piel imperfect of עור (avar, "to be blind") — "it blinds." פִּקְחִים (piqqekhim) = "seeing ones, clear-sighted" — from פקח (paqakh, "to open [the eyes]"). LXX: καὶ δῶρα οὐ λήμψῃ· τὰ γὰρ δῶρα ἐκτυφλοῖ ὀφθαλμούς — δῶρον ("gift") for shokhad.

**9** And a sojourner [ve-ger] you shall not oppress [lo tilkhats], and you [ve-attem] know [yeda'tem] the soul [et-nefesh] of the sojourner [ha-ger], for [ki] sojourners [gerim] you were [heyitem] in the land [be-erets] of Egypt [Mitsrayim].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְגֵר לֹא תִלְחָץ וְאַתֶּם יְדַעְתֶּם אֶת־נֶפֶשׁ הַגֵּר כִּי־גֵרִים הֱיִיתֶם בְּאֶרֶץ מִצְרָיִם. **Key terms:** נֶפֶשׁ הַגֵּר (nefesh ha-ger) = "the soul/life/inner being of the sojourner" — nefesh here means the subjective experience, the felt reality of alienation. יְדַעְתֶּם (yeda'tem) = qal perfect of ידע (yada, "to know") — experiential knowledge, not merely intellectual. LXX: καὶ προσήλυτον οὐ θλίψεσθε· ὑμεῖς γὰρ οἴδατε τὴν ψυχὴν τοῦ προσηλύτου — ψυχή ("soul") for nefesh.

---

## The Sabbath Year and Sabbath Day (vv. 10–12)

**10** And six [ve-shesh] years [shanim] you shall sow [tizra] your land [et-artsekha] and you shall gather in [ve-asafta] its yield [et-tevu'atah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשֵׁשׁ שָׁנִים תִּזְרַע אֶת־אַרְצֶךָ וְאָסַפְתָּ אֶת־תְּבוּאָתָהּ. **Key terms:** תִּזְרַע (tizra) = qal imperfect of זרע (zara, "to sow, scatter seed"). תְּבוּאָתָהּ (tevu'atah) = "its yield, its produce" — from בוא (bo, "to come in"); what the land brings forth. LXX: ἓξ ἔτη σπερεῖς τὴν γῆν σου — σπείρω ("sow") for tizra.

**11** And the seventh [ve-ha-shevi'it] you shall release it [tishmetennah] and you shall abandon it [u-neташtah], and the poor [ve-akhelu evyonei] of your people [ammekha] shall eat [ve-akhelu], and their remainder [ve-yitram] the beast [khayyat] of the field [ha-sadeh] shall eat [tokhal]. So [ken] you shall do [ta'aseh] with your vineyard [le-kharmekha] [and] with your olive grove [le-zeytekha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְהַשְּׁבִיעִת תִּשְׁמְטֶנָּה וּנְטַשְׁתָּהּ וְאָכְלוּ אֶבְיֹנֵי עַמֶּךָ וְיִתְרָם תֹּאכַל חַיַּת הַשָּׂדֶה כֵּן־תַּעֲשֶׂה לְכַרְמְךָ לְזֵיתֶךָ. **Key terms:** תִּשְׁמְטֶנָּה (tishmetennah) = qal imperfect of שׁמט (shamat, "to release, let drop, let fall") — "you shall release it"; the land is released from cultivation. וּנְטַשְׁתָּהּ (u-netashtah) = qal perfect of נטשׁ (natash, "to abandon, leave") — "and you shall abandon it." אֶבְיֹנֵי עַמֶּךָ (evyonei ammekha) = "the needy of your people." LXX: τῷ δὲ ἑβδόμῳ ἄφεσιν ποιήσεις — ἄφεσις ("release") for tishmetennah.

**12** Six [sheshet] days [yamim] you shall do [ta'aseh] your work [ma'asekha], and on the seventh [u-va-yom ha-shevi'i] day [yom] you shall cease [tishbot], so that [lema'an] your ox [shorkha] and your donkey [va-khamorekha] may rest [yanuakh], and the son [ben] of your female servant [amatekha] and the sojourner [ve-ha-ger] may be refreshed [ve-yinnafesh].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads שֵׁשֶׁת יָמִים תַּעֲשֶׂה מַעֲשֶׂיךָ וּבַיּוֹם הַשְּׁבִיעִי תִּשְׁבֹּת לְמַעַן יָנוּחַ שׁוֹרְךָ וַחֲמֹרֶךָ וְיִנָּפֵשׁ בֶּן־אֲמָתְךָ וְהַגֵּר. **Key terms:** תִּשְׁבֹּת (tishbot) = qal imperfect of שׁבת (shavat, "to cease, rest") — the verbal root of "Sabbath." יָנוּחַ (yanuakh) = qal imperfect of נוח (nuakh, "to rest, settle") — physical rest for animals. וְיִנָּפֵשׁ (ve-yinnafesh) = niphal imperfect of נפשׁ (nafash, "to breathe, be refreshed") — denominative from נֶפֶשׁ (nefesh, "soul/breath"); literally "he may re-soul himself." LXX: τῇ δὲ ἡμέρᾳ τῇ ἑβδόμῃ ἀνάπαυσις — ἀνάπαυσις ("rest, cessation") for tishbot.

---

## Warning Against Other Gods (v. 13)

**13** And in all [u-ve-khol] that [asher] I have said [amarti] to you [aleikhem], you shall be watchful [tishhameru], and the name [ve-shem] of other [akherim] gods [elohim] you shall not mention [lo tazkiru]; it shall not be heard [lo yishama] upon [al] your mouth [pikha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וּבְכֹל אֲשֶׁר־אָמַרְתִּי אֲלֵיכֶם תִּשָּׁמֵרוּ וְשֵׁם אֱלֹהִים אֲחֵרִים לֹא תַזְכִּירוּ לֹא יִשָּׁמַע עַל־פִּיךָ. **Key terms:** תִּשָּׁמֵרוּ (tishhameru) = niphal imperfect of שׁמר (shamar, "to keep, guard") — "you shall guard yourselves, be watchful." לֹא תַזְכִּירוּ (lo tazkiru) = hiphil imperfect of זכר (zakhar, "to remember; hiphil: to mention, invoke") — "you shall not cause to be remembered" = "you shall not mention/invoke." לֹא יִשָּׁמַע עַל־פִּיךָ (lo yishama al-pikha) = "it shall not be heard upon your mouth" — the divine name of other gods must not even pass one's lips. LXX: καὶ ὄνομα θεῶν ἑτέρων οὐκ ἀναμνησθήσεσθε — ἀναμιμνῄσκω ("call to mind, mention") for tazkiru.

---

## The Three Pilgrimage Festivals (vv. 14–19)

**14** Three [shalosh] times [regalim] you shall make a pilgrimage-feast [takhog] to me [li] in the year [ba-shanah].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads שָׁלֹשׁ רְגָלִים תָּחֹג לִי בַּשָּׁנָה. **Key terms:** רְגָלִים (regalim) = "feet, times" — from רֶגֶל (regel, "foot"); the term denotes foot-pilgrimages, festivals requiring travel on foot to the sanctuary. תָּחֹג (takhog) = qal imperfect of חגג (khagag, "to celebrate a pilgrimage-feast, to dance") — the root implies festive circling/dancing. LXX: τρεῖς καιροὺς τοῦ ἐνιαυτοῦ ἑορτάσατέ μοι — καιρός ("appointed time") for regalim.

**15** The feast [et-khag] of unleavened bread [ha-matstsot] you shall keep [tishmor]: seven [shiv'at] days [yamim] you shall eat [tokhal] unleavened bread [matstsot] as [ka-asher] I commanded you [tsivvitikha], at the appointed time [le-mo'ed] of the month [khodesh] of Aviv [ha-Aviv], for [ki] in it [vo] you went out [yatsata] from Egypt [mi-Mitsrayim]; and none shall appear [ve-lo yera'u] before my face [panai] empty [reiqam].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אֶת־חַג הַמַּצּוֹת תִּשְׁמֹר שִׁבְעַת יָמִים תֹּאכַל מַצּוֹת כַּאֲשֶׁר צִוִּיתִךָ לְמוֹעֵד חֹדֶשׁ הָאָבִיב כִּי־בוֹ יָצָאתָ מִמִּצְרָיִם וְלֹא־יֵרָאוּ פָנַי רֵיקָם. **Key terms:** הַמַּצּוֹת (ha-matstsot) = "the unleavened breads" — from מַצָּה (matstsah); bread without leaven. הָאָבִיב (ha-Aviv) = "the Aviv" — the month of ripening barley ears (March/April). רֵיקָם (reiqam) = "empty, empty-handed" — one must not appear before YHWH without an offering. LXX: τὴν ἑορτὴν τῶν ἀζύμων φυλάξεσθε — ἄζυμος ("unleavened") for matstsot.

**16** And the feast [ve-khag] of harvest [ha-qatsir], the firstfruits [bikkurei] of your labors [ma'asekha] which [asher] you sow [tizra] in the field [ba-sadeh]; and the feast [ve-khag] of ingathering [ha-asif] at the going out [be-tset] of the year [ha-shanah], when you gather in [be-ospekha] your labors [et-ma'asekha] from [min] the field [ha-sadeh].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְחַג הַקָּצִיר בִּכּוּרֵי מַעֲשֶׂיךָ אֲשֶׁר תִּזְרַע בַּשָּׂדֶה וְחַג הָאָסִף בְּצֵאת הַשָּׁנָה בְּאָסְפְּךָ אֶת־מַעֲשֶׂיךָ מִן־הַשָּׂדֶה. **Key terms:** הַקָּצִיר (ha-qatsir) = "the harvest" — from קצר (qatsar, "to reap, cut short"); the wheat harvest (Shavuot/Weeks). בִּכּוּרֵי (bikkurei) = "firstfruits of" — from בכר (bakhar, "to be firstborn"). הָאָסִף (ha-asif) = "the ingathering" — from אסף (asaf, "to gather in"); the autumn harvest festival (Sukkot/Booths). LXX: καὶ ἑορτὴν θερισμοῦ πρωτογενημάτων — θερισμός ("harvest") for qatsir; πρωτογένημα ("first produce") for bikkurei.

**17** Three [shalosh] times [pe'amim] in the year [ba-shanah] all [kol] your males [zekhurxkha] shall appear [yera'eh] before [el-penei] the face [penei] of the Lord [ha-Adon], YHWH.

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads שָׁלֹשׁ פְּעָמִים בַּשָּׁנָה יֵרָאֶה כָּל־זְכוּרְךָ אֶל־פְּנֵי הָאָדֹן יְהוָה. **Key terms:** פְּעָמִים (pe'amim) = "times, occurrences" — from פעם (pa'am, "a stroke, a time"). זְכוּרְךָ (zekhurxkha) = "your males" — from זכר (zakhar, "male"). הָאָדֹן (ha-Adon) = "the Lord, the Master" — from אדן (adon, "lord, master"); a distinct title from Adonai, emphasizing sovereignty. LXX: τρεῖς καιροὺς τοῦ ἐνιαυτοῦ ὀφθήσεται πᾶν ἀρσενικόν σου ἐνώπιον κυρίου τοῦ θεοῦ — ἀρσενικός ("male") for zekhur.

**18** You shall not slaughter [lo tizbach] upon [al] leavened bread [khamets] the blood [dam] of my sacrifice [zivkhi], and the fat [ve-khelev] of my feast [khaggi] shall not remain overnight [lo yalin] until [ad] morning [boqer].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לֹא־תִזְבַּח עַל־חָמֵץ דַּם־זִבְחִי וְלֹא־יָלִין חֵלֶב־חַגִּי עַד־בֹּקֶר. **Key terms:** חָמֵץ (khamets) = "leaven, leavened bread" — from חמץ (khamats, "to be sour, leavened"). חֵלֶב (khelev) = "fat" — the choicest portion, reserved for YHWH on the altar. לֹא־יָלִין (lo yalin) = "shall not remain overnight" — qal imperfect of לין (lin, "to lodge, pass the night"); sacrificial fat must be burned the same day. LXX: οὐ θύσεις ἐπὶ ζύμῃ αἷμα θυσιάσματός μου — ζύμη ("leaven") for khamets.

**19** The first [reshit] of the firstfruits [bikkurei] of your ground [admatekha] you shall bring [tavi] to the house [beit] of YHWH your God [Elohekha]. You shall not boil [lo tevashel] a kid [gedi] in the milk [ba-khalev] of its mother [immo].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads רֵאשִׁית בִּכּוּרֵי אַדְמָתְךָ תָּבִיא בֵּית יְהוָה אֱלֹהֶיךָ לֹא־תְבַשֵּׁל גְּדִי בַּחֲלֵב אִמּוֹ. **Key terms:** רֵאשִׁית בִּכּוּרֵי (reshit bikkurei) = "the first of the firstfruits" — a double superlative; the very best of the first produce. גְּדִי (gedi) = "a kid, a young goat" — from גדי. בַּחֲלֵב אִמּוֹ (ba-khalev immo) = "in the milk of its mother" — this prohibition (repeated in 34:26 and Deut 14:21) became the foundation of the kashrut separation of meat and dairy. LXX: τὰς ἀπαρχὰς τῶν πρωτογενημάτων τῆς γῆς σου εἰσοίσεις εἰς τὸν οἶκον κυρίου τοῦ θεοῦ σου. οὐχ ἑψήσεις ἄρνα ἐν γάλακτι μητρὸς αὐτοῦ — ἀρήν ("lamb") for gedi, a notable divergence.

---

## The Angel and the Conquest (vv. 20–26)

**20** Behold [hinneh], I [anokhi] am sending [sholeakh] an angel [malakh] before you [lefanekha] to guard you [lishmarkha] on the way [ba-derekh] and to bring you [ve-la-havi'akha] to [el] the place [ha-maqom] which [asher] I have prepared [hakhinoti].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads הִנֵּה אָנֹכִי שֹׁלֵחַ מַלְאָךְ לְפָנֶיךָ לִשְׁמָרְךָ בַּדָּרֶךְ וְלַהֲבִיאֲךָ אֶל־הַמָּקוֹם אֲשֶׁר הֲכִנֹתִי. **Key terms:** מַלְאָךְ (malakh) = "messenger, angel" — from the root לאך (to send a message); the identity of this angel is debated — a created being or a theophanic manifestation of YHWH himself (cf. v. 21, "my name is in his midst"). הֲכִנֹתִי (hakhinoti) = hiphil perfect of כון (kun, "to establish, prepare") — YHWH has already prepared the destination. LXX: καὶ ἰδοὺ ἐγὼ ἀποστέλλω τὸν ἄγγελόν μου πρὸ προσώπου σου — ἄγγελος ("messenger, angel") for malakh.

**21** Be watchful [hishshamer] before him [mippanav] and listen [u-shema] to his voice [be-qolo]; do not be rebellious [al tammer] against him [bo], for [ki] he will not forgive [lo yissa] your transgression [le-fish'akhem], for [ki] my name [shemi] is in his midst [be-qirbo].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads הִשָּׁמֶר מִפָּנָיו וּשְׁמַע בְּקֹלוֹ אַל־תַּמֵּר בּוֹ כִּי לֹא יִשָּׂא לְפִשְׁעֲכֶם כִּי שְׁמִי בְּקִרְבּוֹ. **Key terms:** אַל־תַּמֵּר (al tammer) = hiphil jussive of מרה (marah, "to be rebellious, bitter") — "do not rebel." לֹא יִשָּׂא לְפִשְׁעֲכֶם (lo yissa le-fish'akhem) = "he will not lift up [i.e., forgive] your transgression" — the idiom נשׂא + פשׁע = to bear/forgive transgression. שְׁמִי בְּקִרְבּוֹ (shemi be-qirbo) = "my name is in his midst/interior" — YHWH's name (his essential character and authority) indwells the angel. LXX: εἰσάκουε αὐτοῦ...οὐ γὰρ μὴ ὑποστείληταί σε· τὸ γὰρ ὄνομά μού ἐστιν ἐπ᾽ αὐτῷ — ἐπ᾽ αὐτῷ ("upon him") for be-qirbo.

**22** But [ki] if [im] you surely listen [shamo'a tishma] to his voice [be-qolo] and do [ve-asita] all [kol] that [asher] I speak [adabber], then I will be an enemy [ve-ayavti] to your enemies [et-oyevekha] and an adversary [ve-tsarti] to your adversaries [et-tsorerekha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כִּי אִם־שָׁמוֹעַ תִּשְׁמַע בְּקֹלוֹ וְעָשִׂיתָ כֹּל אֲשֶׁר אֲדַבֵּר וְאָיַבְתִּי אֶת־אֹיְבֶיךָ וְצַרְתִּי אֶת־צֹרְרֶיךָ. **Key terms:** שָׁמוֹעַ תִּשְׁמַע (shamo'a tishma) = infinitive absolute + qal imperfect of שׁמע — "hearing you shall hear" = "if you surely listen." וְאָיַבְתִּי אֶת־אֹיְבֶיךָ (ve-ayavti et-oyevekha) = "and I will be an enemy to your enemies" — YHWH becomes Israel's co-belligerent. וְצַרְתִּי אֶת־צֹרְרֶיךָ (ve-tsarti et-tsorerekha) = "and I will be an adversary to your adversaries." LXX: καὶ ἐχθρανῶ τοῖς ἐχθροῖς σου — ἐχθραίνω ("be hostile to") for ve-ayavti.

**23** For [ki] my angel [malakhi] shall go [yelekh] before you [lefanekha] and bring you [ve-hevi'akha] to [el] the Amorite [ha-Emori] and the Hittite [ve-ha-Khitti] and the Perizzite [ve-ha-Perizzi] and the Canaanite [ve-ha-Kena'ani], the Hivite [ha-Khivvi] and the Jebusite [ve-ha-Yevusi], and I will annihilate them [ve-hikhakhadtiv].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads כִּי־יֵלֵךְ מַלְאָכִי לְפָנֶיךָ וֶהֱבִיאֲךָ אֶל־הָאֱמֹרִי וְהַחִתִּי וְהַפְּרִזִּי וְהַכְּנַעֲנִי הַחִוִּי וְהַיְבוּסִי וְהִכְחַדְתִּיו. **Key terms:** מַלְאָכִי (malakhi) = "my angel/messenger" — with 1cs possessive suffix. וְהִכְחַדְתִּיו (ve-hikhakhadtiv) = hiphil perfect of כחד (kakhad, "to hide, destroy, annihilate") + 3ms suffix — "and I will annihilate him/them." Six nations are listed here (cf. seven in Deut 7:1). LXX: καὶ ἐκτρίψω αὐτούς — ἐκτρίβω ("rub out, destroy utterly") for ve-hikhakhadtiv.

**24** You shall not bow down [lo tishtakhaveh] to their gods [le-eloheihem] and you shall not serve them [ve-lo ta'ovdem], and you shall not do [ve-lo ta'aseh] according to their deeds [ke-ma'aseihem]; but [ki] you shall surely tear down [haros teharsenem] and you shall surely break [ve-shabber teshabbern] their pillars [mattsevoteihem].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לֹא־תִשְׁתַּחֲוֶה לֵאלֹהֵיהֶם וְלֹא תָעָבְדֵם וְלֹא תַעֲשֶׂה כְּמַעֲשֵׂיהֶם כִּי הָרֹס תְּהָרְסֵם וְשַׁבֵּר תְּשַׁבֵּר מַצֵּבֹתֵיהֶם. **Key terms:** הָרֹס תְּהָרְסֵם (haros teharsenem) = infinitive absolute + qal imperfect of הרס (haras, "to tear down, demolish") — "tearing down you shall tear them down." וְשַׁבֵּר תְּשַׁבֵּר מַצֵּבֹתֵיהֶם (ve-shabber teshabber mattsevoteihem) = infinitive absolute + piel imperfect of שׁבר + "their pillars" — "and breaking you shall break their pillars." מַצֵּבוֹת (mattsevot) = standing stones used as cult objects. LXX: ἀλλὰ καθαιρέσει καθελεῖς αὐτοὺς καὶ συντρίβων συντρίψεις τὰς στήλας αὐτῶν — preserves the infinitive absolute doubling.

**25** And you shall serve [va-avadtem] YHWH your God [Elohekhem], and he will bless [u-verakh] your bread [et-lakhmekha] and your water [ve-et-meimekha], and I will turn aside [va-hasiroti] sickness [makhalah] from your midst [mi-qirbekha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וַעֲבַדְתֶּם אֵת יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵיכֶם וּבֵרַךְ אֶת־לַחְמְךָ וְאֶת־מֵימֶיךָ וַהֲסִרֹתִי מַחֲלָה מִקִּרְבֶּךָ. **Key terms:** וּבֵרַךְ (u-verakh) = piel perfect of ברך (barakh, "to bless, kneel") — "and he will bless." Note the shift from third person ("he will bless") to first person ("I will turn aside"), blending the angel's role with YHWH's own action. מַחֲלָה (makhalah) = "sickness, disease" — from חלה (khalah, "to be sick"). LXX: καὶ λατρεύσεις κυρίῳ τῷ θεῷ σου, καὶ εὐλογήσω τὸν ἄρτον σου — εὐλογέω ("bless") for u-verakh; LXX reads first person throughout.

**26** There shall not be [lo tihyeh] one who miscarries [meshakkelah] or [va-aqarah] one who is barren [aqarah] in your land [be-artsekha]; the number [mispar] of your days [yamekha] I will fill [amalle].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לֹא תִהְיֶה מְשַׁכֵּלָה וַעֲקָרָה בְּאַרְצֶךָ אֶת־מִסְפַּר יָמֶיךָ אֲמַלֵּא. **Key terms:** מְשַׁכֵּלָה (meshakkelah) = piel participle feminine of שׁכל (shakhal, "to be bereaved of children, to miscarry") — "one who miscarries/loses children." עֲקָרָה (aqarah) = "barren" — from עקר (aqar, "to uproot; to be barren"). אֲמַלֵּא (amalle) = piel imperfect of מלא (male, "to fill") — "I will fill [full]"; the promise of a complete lifespan. LXX: οὐκ ἔσται ἄγονος οὐδὲ στεῖρα ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς σου — ἄγονος ("childless") for meshakkelah; στεῖρα ("barren") for aqarah.

---

## The Conquest: Terror, Hornets, and Gradual Expulsion (vv. 27–30)

**27** My terror [et-eimati] I will send [ashallakh] before you [lefanekha], and I will throw into confusion [ve-hammoti] all [et-kol] the people [ha-am] among whom [asher] you come [tavo], and I will give [ve-natatti] all [et-kol] your enemies [oyevekha] to you [elekha] [as] the back of the neck [oref].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads אֶת־אֵימָתִי אֲשַׁלַּח לְפָנֶיךָ וְהַמֹּתִי אֶת־כָּל־הָעָם אֲשֶׁר תָּבֹא בָּהֶם וְנָתַתִּי אֶת־כָּל־אֹיְבֶיךָ אֵלֶיךָ עֹרֶף. **Key terms:** אֵימָתִי (eimati) = "my terror, my dread" — from אֵימָה (eimah, "terror, dread"). וְהַמֹּתִי (ve-hammoti) = qal perfect of הום/המם (hamam, "to confuse, throw into panic, discomfit") — divine panic upon the enemy. עֹרֶף (oref) = "back of the neck, nape" — the enemies will turn their backs in flight. LXX: καὶ τὸν φόβον ἀποστελῶ ἡγούμενόν σου — φόβος ("fear") for eimati.

**28** And I will send [ve-shalakhti] the hornet [et-ha-tsir'ah] before you [lefanekha], and it will drive out [ve-gereshash] the Hivite [et-ha-Khivvi], the Canaanite [et-ha-Kena'ani], and the Hittite [ve-et-ha-Khitti] from before you [mi-lefanekha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשָׁלַחְתִּי אֶת־הַצִּרְעָה לְפָנֶיךָ וְגֵרְשָׁה אֶת־הַחִוִּי אֶת־הַכְּנַעֲנִי וְאֶת־הַחִתִּי מִלְּפָנֶיךָ. **Key terms:** הַצִּרְעָה (ha-tsir'ah) = "the hornet" — from צרעה (tsir'ah); possibly literal hornets, or metaphorical for panic/plague/Egypt itself. The definite article ("the hornet") suggests a known instrument of divine warfare. וְגֵרְשָׁה (ve-gereshash) = piel perfect of גרשׁ (garash, "to drive out, cast out") — the hornet as agent of expulsion. LXX: καὶ ἀποστελῶ τὰς σφηκίας πρὸ προσώπου σου — σφηκία ("wasps") for tsir'ah, plural.

**29** I will not drive them out [lo agareshennu] from before you [mi-panekha] in one [be-shanah ekhat] year [shanah], lest [pen] the land [ha-arets] become [tihyeh] a desolation [shemamah] and the beast [khayyat] of the field [ha-sadeh] multiply [ve-rabbah] against you [alekha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לֹא אֲגָרְשֶׁנּוּ מִפָּנֶיךָ בְּשָׁנָה אֶחָת פֶּן־תִּהְיֶה הָאָרֶץ שְׁמָמָה וְרַבָּה עָלֶיךָ חַיַּת הַשָּׂדֶה. **Key terms:** שְׁמָמָה (shemamah) = "desolation, waste" — from שׁמם (shamam, "to be desolate, appalled"). The ecological reasoning is striking: too-rapid conquest would leave the land depopulated and wild animals would overrun it. YHWH's timetable accounts for the land's carrying capacity. LXX: οὐκ ἐκβαλῶ αὐτοὺς ἐν ἐνιαυτῷ ἑνί — ἐκβάλλω ("cast out") for agareshennu.

**30** Little [me'at] by little [me'at] I will drive them out [agareshennu] from before you [mi-panekha], until [ad] that [asher] you are fruitful [tifreh] and you inherit [ve-nakhalta] the land [et-ha-arets].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads מְעַט מְעַט אֲגָרְשֶׁנּוּ מִפָּנֶיךָ עַד אֲשֶׁר תִּפְרֶה וְנָחַלְתָּ אֶת־הָאָרֶץ. **Key terms:** מְעַט מְעַט (me'at me'at) = "little [by] little" — the repetition expresses gradual progression. תִּפְרֶה (tifreh) = qal imperfect of פרה (parah, "to be fruitful, bear fruit") — echoing the creation blessing of Gen 1:28. וְנָחַלְתָּ (ve-nakhalta) = qal perfect of נחל (nakhal, "to inherit, take possession of") — permanent inheritance, not temporary occupation. LXX: κατὰ μικρὸν μικρὸν ἐκβαλῶ αὐτούς — κατὰ μικρόν ("little by little") for me'at me'at.

---

## The Boundaries and the Covenant Warning (vv. 31–33)

**31** And I will set [ve-shatti] your border [et-gevulekha] from the Sea [mi-Yam] of Reeds [Suf] and unto [ve-ad] the Sea [Yam] of the Philistines [Pelishtim], and from the wilderness [u-mi-midbar] unto [ad] the River [ha-nahar]; for [ki] I will give [etten] into your hand [be-yedkhem] the inhabitants [et-yoshevei] of the land [ha-arets], and you shall drive them out [ve-geirashto] from before you [mi-panekha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads וְשַׁתִּי אֶת־גְּבֻלְךָ מִיַּם־סוּף וְעַד־יָם פְּלִשְׁתִּים וּמִמִּדְבָּר עַד־הַנָּהָר כִּי אֶתֵּן בְּיֶדְכֶם אֵת יֹשְׁבֵי הָאָרֶץ וְגֵרַשְׁתָּמוֹ מִפָּנֶיךָ. **Key terms:** וְשַׁתִּי (ve-shatti) = qal perfect of שׁית (shit, "to set, place, put") — "and I will set/establish." יַּם־סוּף (Yam-Suf) = "Sea of Reeds" — the southern boundary. יָם פְּלִשְׁתִּים (Yam Pelishtim) = "Sea of the Philistines" — the Mediterranean, the western boundary. הַנָּהָר (ha-nahar) = "the River" — the Euphrates, the eastern/northeastern boundary. LXX: καὶ θήσω τὰ ὅριά σου ἀπὸ τῆς ἐρυθρᾶς θαλάσσης ἕως τῆς θαλάσσης τῆς Φυλιστιιμ — ἐρυθρὰ θάλασσα ("Red Sea") for Yam-Suf.

**32** You shall not cut [lo tikhrot] for them [lahem] or for their gods [ve-le-eloheihem] a covenant [berit].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לֹא־תִכְרֹת לָהֶם וְלֵאלֹהֵיהֶם בְּרִית. **Key terms:** לֹא־תִכְרֹת (lo tikhrot) = qal imperfect of כרת (karat, "to cut") — "you shall not cut"; covenants are "cut" because the ceremony involved cutting animals (cf. Gen 15:10). בְּרִית (berit) = "covenant" — a binding agreement; making a treaty with Canaanite nations or their gods would compromise exclusive loyalty to YHWH. LXX: οὐ συγκαταθήσῃ αὐτοῖς καὶ τοῖς θεοῖς αὐτῶν διαθήκην — διαθήκη ("covenant, testament") for berit.

**33** They shall not dwell [lo yeshvu] in your land [be-artsekha], lest [pen] they cause you to sin [yakhtiu otekha] against me [li], for [ki] if you serve [ta'avod] their gods [et-eloheihem], surely [ki] it will be [yihyeh] a snare [le-moqesh] to you [lekha].

> **Manuscript note:** LC reads לֹא יֵשְׁבוּ בְּאַרְצְךָ פֶּן־יַחֲטִיאוּ אֹתְךָ לִי כִּי תַעֲבֹד אֶת־אֱלֹהֵיהֶם כִּי־יִהְיֶה לְךָ לְמוֹקֵשׁ. **Key terms:** יַחֲטִיאוּ (yakhtiu) = hiphil imperfect of חטא (khata, "to miss, sin") — "they will cause you to sin"; the hiphil makes the Canaanite inhabitants the agents of Israel's transgression. לְמוֹקֵשׁ (le-moqesh) = "for a snare, a trap" — from יקשׁ (yaqash, "to lay a snare"); the word evokes a bird-trap that ensnares the unwary. The chapter — and the entire Book of the Covenant — ends on this warning: cohabitation with idolaters will become an inescapable trap. LXX: οὐκ ἐγκαθήσονται ἐν τῇ γῇ σου, ἵνα μὴ ἁμαρτεῖν σε ποιήσωσιν πρός με — ἁμαρτάνω ("sin") for yakhtiu; the LXX renders the causative sense with ποιέω ("cause to do").

---

## Translator's Afterword

Exodus 23 closes the Book of the Covenant with a sweeping arc from courtroom to calendar to conquest. The first nine verses establish that justice is not merely procedural but relational — rooted in Israel's experiential knowledge of what it means to be a stranger (v. 9, "you know the soul of the sojourner"). The Sabbath laws (vv. 10–12) extend the principle of rest from humans to animals to the land itself, with the rare verb yinnafesh (v. 12, "may re-soul himself") grounding physical rest in the renewal of the nefesh. The festival calendar (vv. 14–19) binds Israel's agricultural life to the rhythm of pilgrimage, culminating in the enigmatic kid-in-milk prohibition that closes the cultic section.

Key textual observations across the MT-LXX comparison: (1) In v. 19, the LXX reads ἀρήν ("lamb") where the MT has gedi ("kid"), a notable lexical divergence. (2) In v. 21, the LXX renders be-qirbo ("in his midst") as ἐπ᾽ αὐτῷ ("upon him"), flattening the spatial metaphor of YHWH's name dwelling inside the angel. (3) In v. 28, the LXX pluralizes "hornet" to σφηκίαι ("wasps"), interpreting the collective singular as a swarm. (4) In v. 31, the LXX renders Yam-Suf as ἐρυθρὰ θάλασσα ("Red Sea"), the well-known mistranslation that entered Western tradition — the Hebrew means "Sea of Reeds," not "Red Sea." (5) The two infinitive absolute pairs in v. 24 (haros teharsenem, shabber teshabber) are faithfully preserved in the LXX with cognate participial constructions, a relatively rare instance of the LXX maintaining this distinctively Hebrew emphatic form.

The theological climax of the chapter — and of the entire covenant code — is the angel passage (vv. 20–23). The statement "my name is in his midst" (v. 21) creates a figure who is simultaneously distinct from YHWH and yet bears YHWH's own authority and unforgiving holiness. This angel mediates YHWH's presence in a way that anticipates later biblical theology of divine name-bearing, where the Name functions as a portable mode of divine presence that can indwell a place, a person, or an institution. The conditional structure of vv. 22–33 — obedience yields blessing, disobedience yields the snare — establishes the covenantal pattern that will govern the rest of Israel's story through Joshua, Judges, and Kings.
